raw_output_payload.reasoning:
We need a bash command to count unique file extensions in /testbed. Use find, basename, cut, sort, uniq, wc -l. Exclude directories. For files with no extension? maybe ignore. Command: find /testbed -type f -printf "%f\n" | rev | cut -d. -f1 | rev | grep -v '^$' | sort -u | wc -l

Simplify: find /testbed -type f -printf "%f\n" | awk -F. 'NF>1{print $NF}' | sort -u | wc -l

Return one line.

Simplify: find /testbed -type f -printf "%f\n" | awk -F. 'NF>1{print $NF}' | sort -u | wc -l

Return one line.

task.query: Count the number of unique file extensions in the /testbed directory tree
task.gold: find /testbed -type f | sed -e 's/.*\.//' | sed -e 's/.*\///' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

record.query: Count the number of unique file extensions in the /testbed directory tree
record.gold: find /testbed -type f | sed -e 's/.*\.//' | sed -e 's/.*\///' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

reset.observation: Count the number of unique file extensions in the /testbed directory tree
